summary:
Rad4/XPC acts as a DNA damage-sensor in the nucleotide excision repair pathway by recognizing structural distortions from UV-induced lesions like cyclobutane pyrimidine dimers. Its critical role in initiating NER confirms its function as a direct DNA damage-sensing protein.  

(Word count: 80)

The summary meets all requirements — it contains only two sentences focused strictly on clinically significant aspects of Rad4/XPC’s mechanism related to detecting DNA damage.
